# Reliability Checklist: Unified landing page and IA shell

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-002 reliability requirements for docs-site command scripts, build and link-validation failure modes, Pages-ready configuration, and deterministic completion gates.
**Created**: 2026-06-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Command Script Requirements

- [x] CHK001 Are all docs-site-scoped `pnpm` command roles required by the spec represented as package scripts runnable after changing into `docs-site/`? [Resolved, Spec FR-002, Plan Selected Command Roles, Data Model Validation Command Role, Contract commands]
- [x] CHK002 Are the exact script bodies for `check`, `build`, `validate`, `validate:links`, `preview`, and `dev` specified enough for implementation without guessing? [Resolved, Plan Selected Command Roles, Data Model Validation Command Role, Contract packageScripts]
- [x] CHK003 Are docs-site validation commands clearly separated from root plugin structural tests unless plugin or SpecKit scaffold surfaces are touched? [Resolved, Spec SC-006, Plan Testing, Tasks T032-T035]

## Build And Link-Validation Failure Modes

- [x] CHK004 Are production build failure classes documented, including Astro/Starlight config errors, content/schema errors, route generation errors, and Pages base/path mismatches? [Resolved, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Data Model Validation Command Role, Contract failureModes]
- [x] CHK005 Are internal-link validation failures scoped to Markdown/MDX route links and slug/base-path issues, with external URL availability excluded from the minimum gate? [Resolved, Spec FR-022, Research local deterministic gate decision, Quickstart Internal-Link Validation]
- [x] CHK006 Are failure outcomes clear enough that maintainers know which failures block DOC-002 completion versus which broader docs hardening remains deferred to DOC-010? [Resolved, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Contract failureModes, Spec Assumptions]

## Pages-Ready Configuration

- [x] CHK007 Are Pages-ready configuration assumptions stated with concrete `site`, `base`, and `trailingSlash` values even though DOC-002 must not add a GitHub Pages publish workflow? [Resolved, Plan Phase 0 Decisions, Research Pages decision, Contract pagesConfig]
- [x] CHK008 Are Pages assumptions represented consistently across spec, plan, data model, contract, quickstart, and workflow handoff language? [Resolved, Spec FR-013, Plan Phase 0 Decisions, Data Model Documentation Site, Contract pagesConfig]
- [x] CHK009 Is the no-publish-workflow boundary stated as a reliability requirement rather than only as implementation scope guidance? [Resolved, Spec FR-014, Data Model Documentation Site, Quickstart Prerequisites]

## Deterministic Minimum Gate

- [x] CHK010 Does the spec define DOC-002's minimum completion gate as local and deterministic, avoiding browser screenshots, external-link crawls, deployment checks, or other network-dependent validation? [Resolved, Spec FR-022, Spec SC-011, Research local deterministic gate decision, Contract minimumCompletionGate]
- [x] CHK011 Are dependency installation and lockfile generation distinguished from the repeatable completion gate so validation after scaffold does not depend on live package-network access? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Quickstart Setup]
- [x] CHK012 Are broader reliability checks such as docs CI, external-link policy, search hardening, responsive screenshots, and accessibility automation explicitly deferred to DOC-010? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Research local deterministic gate decision, Contract deferredToDoc010]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 Can a reviewer objectively verify docs-site build and internal-link validation success from documented commands and expected failure boundaries? [Resolved, Spec SC-006, Quickstart, Tasks T032-T035]
- [x] CHK014 Are reliability requirements traceable across the spec, plan, research, data model, route-shell contract, quickstart, and workflow Checklist Results row? [Resolved, Workflow Checklist Results, Plan Selected Command Roles, Contract commands, Quickstart]

## Verification Run 2

- [x] CHK015 Are package script bodies now explicit in both the plan and route-shell contract? [Resolved, Plan Selected Command Roles, Contract packageScripts]
- [x] CHK016 Are build and internal-link validation failure boundaries now documented without treating ordinary config errors as framework-selection blockers? [Resolved, Plan Reliability Failure Modes, Research local deterministic gate decision]
- [x] CHK017 Are GitHub Pages `site`, `base`, and `trailingSlash` assumptions now consistent across spec, plan, data model, contract, quickstart, and workflow results? [Resolved, Spec FR-013, Data Model Documentation Site, Contract pagesConfig, Quickstart Full DOC-002 Validation]
- [x] CHK018 Does the minimum completion gate now exclude deployment, screenshots, analytics checks, external-link crawling, remote URL availability checks, and post-install package-network access? [Resolved, Spec FR-022, Spec SC-011, Contract minimumCompletionGate]
- [x] CHK019 Are dependency installation and lockfile refresh now documented as setup rather than the repeatable DOC-002 completion gate? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Quickstart Setup]
